```python
class Julian2DateUtil:
    @staticmethod
    def convert(year_digit: str, julian_day: str) -> 'Date':
        year = 2000 + int(year_digit)
        day_of_year = int(julian_day)

        # Check for leap year
        is_leap = (year % 4 == 0 and year % 100 != 0) or (year % 400 == 0)

        # Days in each month
        days_in_month = [31, 28 + int(is_leap), 31, 30, 31, 30, 31, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31]

        month = 0
        for days in days_in_month:
            if day_of_year <= days:
                break
            day_of_year -= days
            month += 1

        return Date(year, month + 1, day_of_year)

# Minimal unit tests
def test_julian2dateutil():
    assert Julian2DateUtil.convert('1', '001') == Date(2011, 1, 1)
    assert Julian2DateUtil.convert('2', '060') == Date(2012, 2, 29)  # Leap year
    assert Julian2DateUtil.convert('1', '365') == Date(2011, 12, 31)
    assert Julian2DateUtil.convert('2', '059') == Date(2012, 2, 28)  # Non-leap year
    assert Julian2DateUtil.convert('3', '001') == Date(2013, 1, 1)

# Adversarial/edge-case tests
def test_edge_cases():
    assert Julian2DateUtil.convert('1', '000') == Date(2011, 1, 1)  # Invalid day, should default to first day
    assert Julian2DateUtil.convert('2', '366') == Date(2012, 12, 31)  # Invalid day, should default to last day of leap year

# Run tests
test_julian2dateutil()
test_edge_cases()
```
